title: BACKND Base player data flows
summary: Describes per-player data storage in BACKND Base, including inserts, reads, updates, calculations, deletes, and transaction operations for rows that belong to a specific user context.
capabilities: route personal progression and inventory persistence questions to the right document family
explain that rows are tied to the inserting user and carry reserved ownership fields
surface data-shape and capacity constraints that affect insert and update behavior
identify when transaction read or write patterns should be considered
prerequisites: A logged-in player context exists for normal player-data flows
The destination table exists and matches the intended schema rules
The caller understands whether the row belongs to the current player or must be queried differently
common_pitfalls: Confusing player-data with shared game-information storage
Writing reserved fields as if they were normal application columns
Ignoring schema, list, map, and large-number constraints when generating insert or update payloads
public_notes: Player-data guidance should stay explicit about ownership semantics because that affects both retrieval and mutation logic.
Agents should confirm exact method names and payload constraints from the official docs before generating production code.
